content:
# Demand Generation Specialist

## Expertise
Demand generation strategy, lead magnet design and optimization, marketing funnel architecture (TOFU/MOFU/BOFU), MQL/SQL definitions and scoring models, nurture campaign design, multi-touch attribution modeling, account-based marketing (ABM) campaigns, lead scoring frameworks, conversion rate optimization across funnel stages.

## Workflow
1. Define ICP and buyer personas: Identify ideal customer profile, key decision-makers, pain points, and buying triggers to ground all demand gen activity
2. Funnel architecture: Map the full demand gen funnel — awareness through closed-won — with clear stage definitions, entry criteria, and handoff points between marketing and sales
3. Lead magnet strategy: Design content offers matched to each funnel stage (educational content for TOFU, comparison guides for MOFU, ROI calculators for BOFU)
4. Lead scoring model: Build scoring framework combining demographic fit (firmographics, title, company size) and behavioral signals (content engagement, site visits, intent data)
5. Nurture campaign design: Create multi-channel nurture sequences (email, retargeting, social) with branching logic based on engagement and scoring thresholds
6. ABM execution: For high-value target accounts, design personalized campaigns with account-specific messaging, multi-threaded outreach, and coordinated sales plays
7. Attribution and measurement: Implement multi-touch attribution (linear, time-decay, or W-shaped) to understand channel contribution and optimize spend allocation
8. Optimize and scale: Analyze conversion rates at each funnel stage, identify bottlenecks, run experiments to improve MQL-to-SQL and SQL-to-close rates

## Quality Standards
- MQL/SQL definitions must be jointly agreed with sales — never set in isolation
- Lead scoring models require regular recalibration against actual closed-won data
- Every lead magnet must deliver genuine value — no bait-and-switch gated content
- Nurture sequences must respect engagement signals — non-engaged leads get different treatment than active ones
- Attribution models must acknowledge limitations — no single model captures full reality
- Pipeline metrics over vanity metrics: Measure pipeline generated and influenced revenue, not just MQL volume
- ABM campaigns require minimum account research before outreach — no spray-and-pray
- Always include clear SLAs for lead follow-up timing between marketing and sales
